title: Extract complex_abs helper to deduplicate magnitude/complex_magnitude
analysis: The `complex_magnitude` match arm (lines 427-437) duplicates the Complex branch of `magnitude` (lines 285-291) verbatim. Both compute `(re*re + im*im).sqrt()` then branch on `dimension == DimensionVector::DIMENSIONLESS` to return either `sanitize_value(Value::Real(mag))` or `sanitize_value(Value::Scalar { si_value: mag, dimension })`. The fix is to extract a private `#[inline(always)] fn complex_abs(re: f64, im: f64, dimension: DimensionVector) -> Value` helper placed in the private helpers section (after `unary` at line 653, before `normalize_quaternion` at line 655). Both call sites then delegate to `complex_abs`. Additionally, we add a `complex_magnitude_overflow_returns_undef` test following the established pattern from `complex_add_overflow_returns_undef` (line 3309) and `complex_mul_overflow_returns_undef` (line 3297). The new test goes in the `// ── complex_magnitude() tests (step-5)` section before line 3391 (the closing brace). All changes are confined to a single file: `crates/reify-stdlib/src/lib.rs`.

steps.description: Write `complex_magnitude_overflow_returns_undef` test in the `// ── complex_magnitude() tests (step-5)` section (before line 3391). Pattern: create `Value::Complex { re: f64::MAX, im: f64::MAX, dimension: DimensionVector::DIMENSIONLESS }`, call `eval_builtin("complex_magnitude", &[z])`, assert `.is_undef()` with message 'complex_magnitude with f64::MAX components must return Undef (Inf overflow)'. This parallels `complex_add_overflow_returns_undef` at line 3309. The test should pass immediately (sanitize_value already catches Inf from f64::MAX²+f64::MAX²→Inf), establishing the behavioral contract before refactoring.

Extract `#[inline(always)] fn complex_abs(re: f64, im: f64, dimension: DimensionVector) -> Value` helper. Place it after `unary()` (line 653) and before `normalize_quaternion()` (line 655). Body: `let mag = (re * re + im * im).sqrt(); if dimension == DimensionVector::DIMENSIONLESS { sanitize_value(Value::Real(mag)) } else { sanitize_value(Value::Scalar { si_value: mag, dimension }) }`. Then replace the `magnitude` Complex branch (lines 285-291) with `return complex_abs(*re, *im, *dimension);` and replace the `complex_magnitude` match arm body (lines 428-434) with `complex_abs(*re, *im, *dimension)`. Run `cargo test -p reify-stdlib` to verify all existing tests plus the new overflow test pass.

Final verification: run `cargo test -p reify-stdlib` confirming all tests pass including: complex_magnitude_3_4_returns_5, complex_magnitude_dimensioned_returns_scalar, complex_magnitude_non_complex_returns_undef, complex_magnitude_voltage, complex_magnitude_overflow_returns_undef, and all magnitude tests. This confirms the refactor is behavior-preserving.

design_decisions.decision: Name the helper `complex_abs` rather than `complex_magnitude_inner` or similar
design_decisions.rationale: The operation is mathematically the complex absolute value (modulus). `complex_abs` is concise, standard mathematical terminology, and clearly conveys intent. It also distinguishes from the public-facing `complex_magnitude` builtin name.

design_decisions.decision: Take `re: f64, im: f64, dimension: DimensionVector` by value rather than by reference
design_decisions.rationale: All three types are Copy (f64 is 8 bytes, DimensionVector is a [Rational; 9] which derives Copy). Passing by value avoids lifetime noise at call sites where we destructure `&Value::Complex { re, im, dimension }` and would otherwise need to dereference. Callers pass `*re, *im, *dimension`.

design_decisions.decision: Place helper between `unary` and `normalize_quaternion` in the private helpers section
design_decisions.rationale: This groups it with other private computation helpers rather than inside the `eval_builtin` match block. It follows the existing code organization pattern where `sanitize_value`, `unary`, `binary`, `normalize_quaternion` etc. are all private fns after the main dispatch function.

design_decisions.decision: Mark as `#[inline(always)]` per the task description
design_decisions.rationale: The function is tiny (a sqrt + a branch + sanitize_value call) and called from two hot paths in `eval_builtin`. Inlining avoids function call overhead and matches the task specification.

design_decisions.decision: Write the overflow test first even though it passes immediately
design_decisions.rationale: The test validates that the sanitize_value defense-in-depth continues to work for complex_magnitude after the refactor. Writing it first establishes the behavioral contract before we touch the implementation. f64::MAX² + f64::MAX² overflows to Inf, which sanitize_value converts to Undef.

reuse.how: Called inside `complex_abs` to convert non-finite results (NaN, Inf) to Undef, exactly as both current call sites already do.
Both `magnitude` and `complex_magnitude` already use `unary(args, |v| ...)` — this stays unchanged; only the closure body changes to delegate to `complex_abs`.
The new `complex_magnitude_overflow_returns_undef` test follows the identical pattern: construct Complex with f64::MAX components, call eval_builtin, assert `.is_undef()` with descriptive message.
Used in both the helper function (for the dimensionless branch) and in the test (for constructing the test Complex value).
